new_classes.definition_en: A spirit produced exclusively in pot stills at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash made only from malted barley; classified under CN subheading 2208 30 30.

new_classes.label_en: Pot still distillation process (single malt whisky production)
new_classes.label_de: Destillation im Pot Still (Herstellungsverfahren für Single-Malt-Whisky)
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process using traditional pot stills (batch distillation vessels) at a single distillery, used in the production of single malt whisky from a fermented mash of only malted barley.

new_classes.label_en: Malted barley mash (fermented mash made exclusively from malted barley)
new_classes.label_de: Maische aus gemälzter Gerste (vergorene Maische ausschließlich aus gemälzter Gerste)
new_classes.definition_en: A fermented mash made exclusively from malted barley, used as the sole raw material in the production of single malt whisky.

new_classes.label_en: Single distillery origin quality (spirit produced entirely at one distillery)
new_classes.label_de: Einzel-Brennerei-Herkunftseigenschaft (ausschließlich in einer einzigen Brennerei hergestellt)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a spirit indicating that the entire production took place at a single distillery, as required for single malt whisky classification.

new_classes.label_en: Malted-barley-only mash quality (fermented mash composed exclusively of malted barley)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality indicating that the fermented mash used in production consists solely of malted barley, with no other grains or adjuncts, as required for single malt whisky.

coverage_explanation: All four principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) production via pot stills modelled as PotStillDistillationProcess (subclass of DistillationProcess2208); (2) single distillery provenance modelled as SingleDistilleryOriginQuality; (3) exclusive use of malted barley mash modelled via MaltedBarleyMash (has part) and MaltedBarleyOnlyMashQuality (has quality); (4) the spirit character captured via parent class SpiritsBeverages2208. A small gap (score -0.08) remains: the legal text implicitly refers to 'Scotch whisky' (i.e. Scottish origin and maturation/ageing requirements under EU GI rules), which are not modelled here because no GeographicArea individual for Scotland/UK or an ageing-quality class is present in the running TBox, and introducing a geographic-origin quality without a corresponding TBox individual would over-commit. The disjointness axiom on PotStillDistillationProcess vs GrapeWineOrMarcDistillationProcess was removed from restrictions as it was modelled incorrectly (complement on a process class is not appropriate via RO_0000086); sibling disjointness is captured structurally through the class hierarchy.
